sourceMovie.title: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Lara Croft: Tomb Raider for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Simon West keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Forbidden Kingdom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lara Croft: Tomb Raider clicked for you, The Forbidden Kingdom (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Librarian: Return to King Solomon's Mines

Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lara Croft: Tomb Raider?
faq.answer: Try The Forbidden Kingdom, The Librarian: Return to King Solomon's Mines, Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1—each shares DNA with Lara Croft: Tomb Raider while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lara Croft: Tomb Raider?
faq.answer: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider (2001) is best described as Adventure, Action, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lara Croft: Tomb Raider part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Tomb Raider Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
